articles.title: The Incidence of Subtalar Facet Penetration and Its Impact on Clinical and Radiographic Outcome in Lateral Lengthening Calcaneal Osteotomies.
articles.authors: Scheurer FA, Suter D, Fritz B, Smolle MA, Beeler S, Viehofer A, et al.
articles.population: Patients aged 18 years or older who underwent lateral lengthening calcaneal osteotomy for progressive collapsing foot deformity
articles.outcomes: Subtalar facet penetration on postoperative CT scan; cartilage degeneration on MRI; Foot Function Index and American Orthopaedic Foot and Ankle Society scores at minimum 5-year follow-up
articles.take_home: About one-third of patients had bone cuts that went into the subtalar joint during surgery, but this did not lead to worse pain, function, or cartilage damage at 5 years compared to those without this complication. Minor violations of the joint may not have lasting clinical importance.

articles.title: Patient-Reported Outcomes and Satisfaction Results on a Modern Fixed-Bearing Total Ankle Arthroplasty: Updated Mean 6-Year Follow-up.
articles.authors: Lang DL, McReavy BR, Moen RK, Stone McGaver R, Fritz JE, Seiffert KJ, et al.
articles.population: Patients with end-stage ankle arthritis undergoing fixed-bearing total ankle arthroplasty
articles.sample_size: 300 patients (321 procedures), 282 patients with minimum 2-year follow-up
articles.outcomes: Physical quality of life, pain, and disability measured using Veterans RAND 12-Item Health Survey, Ankle Osteoarthritis Scale, and visual analog pain scale
articles.take_home: Patients who received a fixed-bearing ankle replacement showed significant improvements in physical quality of life, pain and disability that were sustained at an average of 6 years after surgery. Mental health scores did not change significantly after surgery.

articles.title: Mechanical integrity of a flexor hallucis longus tendon transfer using an all-inside endoscopic approach.
articles.authors: Wei Y, Martin KD, Smith AGF, Piscitani F, Bates NA
articles.outcomes: Failure strength of the flexor hallucis longus tendon construct measured in Newtons
articles.take_home: This new keyhole surgery technique for transferring the flexor hallucis longus tendon to repair chronic Achilles rupture showed the tendon repair was as strong or stronger than traditional methods, whilst potentially avoiding damage to nerves and blood vessels.

articles.title: Defining the posteromedial approach to the ankle: A systematic review of anatomical corridors, nomenclature and surgical exposure.
articles.authors: Fernandez-Rojas E, Osorio-Torres W, Arias-Alvarez G, Bastias GF, Herrera-Perez M, Fletcher L, et al.
articles.outcomes: Anatomical corridors, nomenclature, surgical exposure, functional outcomes and complications
articles.take_home: Surgeons use different names and techniques for posteromedial ankle approaches, and while four main anatomical pathways exist, there is no agreed standard. Current evidence does not clearly show which approach is best.

articles.title: Intercuneiform stabilization during a modified Lapidus procedure for hallux valgus results in decreased intercuneiform gapping and recurrence rates.
articles.authors: Zeng AI, Kim J, Nguyen J, Ellis S, Conti M
articles.population: Patients undergoing modified Lapidus procedure for hallux valgus
articles.sample_size: 171 patients (87 without intercuneiform screw, 84 with intercuneiform screw)
articles.outcomes: Hallux valgus recurrence, intercuneiform gapping, hallux valgus angle, first metatarsal pronation, and sesamoid position
articles.take_home: Adding an extra screw between the first metatarsal and middle cuneiform during hallux valgus surgery reduced deformity recurrence to zero compared with 7% in patients without this extra screw, and improved several measures of foot alignment.

articles.title: Clinical and Radiographic Outcomes at a Mean of 7 Years Following Primary Transfibular Total Ankle Arthroplasty in Younger and Older Patients.
articles.authors: Schafer KA, Day J, Motsay M, Zhang H, Zhang Z, Schon LC
articles.population: Patients undergoing primary transfibular total ankle arthroplasty; younger group mean age 45.4 years, older group mean age 65.7 years
articles.sample_size: 251 ankles in 236 patients (72 ankles in younger group, 179 ankles in older group)
articles.outcomes: Implant survivorship, patient-reported outcomes (SF-12, Ankle Osteoarthritis Scale, pain scale), radiographic alignment, range of motion, periprosthetic cysts, adverse events, and reoperations at mean 7 years follow-up
articles.take_home: Transfibular total ankle replacement performed similarly well in younger and older patients at 7 years, with no significant differences in implant survival, complications, or patient-reported outcomes. This suggests the procedure may be a suitable option for treating end-stage ankle arthritis across a wide age range, though longer follow-up is still needed.

articles.title: No difference in residual laxity following all types of arthroscopic treatment of chronic ankle instability: A meta-analysis.
articles.population: Patients with chronic ankle instability undergoing arthroscopic surgery
articles.outcomes: Residual ankle laxity measured by talar tilt angle and anterior talar translation at minimum 2 years follow-up
articles.take_home: All types of arthroscopic surgery for chronic ankle instability produced similar results in terms of reducing abnormal ankle movement. The choice of surgical technique may matter less than individual biological and patient factors in determining how well the ankle stabilises after surgery.
